title: Karatsuba vs naive cross-verification with property-based tests
description:
Property-based tests confirming Karatsuba (QuadraticExt, 3M) and Karatsuba-style (CubicExt, 6M) multiplication produce identical results to naive schoolbook polynomial multiplication.

## Deliverable

A single integration test file `crates/gf2-core/tests/karatsuba_cross_verify.rs` containing:

- Standalone naive reference implementations (4M schoolbook for quadratic, 9M schoolbook for cubic) that are visibly independent of the optimized code.
- Proptest-based cross-verification with 10000 random element pairs per configuration.
- Multiple field sizes and non-residue choices:
  - QuadraticExt: Fp<7>/β=−1, Fp<7>/β=3, Fp<101>/β=−2, Fp<65537>/β=3
  - CubicExt: Fp<7>/β=3, Fp<31>/β=11, Fp<101>/β=2
- Squaring consistency: `a.square() == naive_mul(a, a)` for each config.

No library code changes. Operation counts documented on existing `mul` impls.

See design plan: `dev/plans/karatsuba_cross_verification.md`

## Success criteria

- 10000+ proptest cases per configuration, all passing.
- Both QuadraticExt and CubicExt covered across multiple primes and non-residues.
- Naive implementations use schoolbook (4M / 9M), not Karatsuba.
